# function: dirmap

Recursively reads YAML (.yaml/.yml) and JSON (.json) files from the given directory (and subdirectories), merges them into a nested map using relative file paths as keys (without extension), and returns the entire structure serialized as a JSON string.

Use `jsondecode(lumberyard::dirmap(...))` to get a Terraform object/map.



## Signature

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
dirmap(path string, filter string) string
```

## Arguments

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `path` (String) Absolute or relative path to the base directory to traverse. Must exist and be readable.
1. `filter` (String) Glob pattern to match filenames (e.g. "**/*.yaml", "config/*.json"). Use empty string `""` to include all supported files.
